title: Automate post-task handoff and context compaction
summary: Design and implement a safe post-task handoff flow that persists next focus, writes compact resume context, and integrates Pi/VCC/native compaction hooks after commit or push without unsafe automatic pushes by default.

goal.outcome: After closing and pushing a task, CodeWiki/Pi can start a fresh or compacted session from persisted handoff context with minimal token waste and no hidden chat dependency.
acceptance: A post-task handoff mechanism persists next task focus and a compact resume packet after successful task closure/checkpoint.
Pi integration point for VCC/native compaction is identified and implemented or documented with a safe fallback.
Default behavior does not automatically push or perform irreversible actions without explicit user opt-in/config.
Smoke or characterization coverage proves resume after handoff uses compact persisted context rather than stale chat state.
non_goals: Do not make automatic remote push the default behavior.
Do not replace CodeWiki task packs with chat transcript summaries as source of truth.
Do not couple core CodeWiki to Pi-only compaction APIs.
verification: Run codewiki_state refresh=true and confirm green health.

delta.desired: Task boundaries produce compact, persisted handoff context and optional Pi compaction/session reset automation.
delta.current: Agents rely on manual reload/new-session steps and may waste tokens or resume from stale branch-local state.
delta.closure: Post-task handoff and compaction path is implemented or explicitly staged with tested integration seams.
